title: Evaluating Image Captions: Metrics
concept: Automated caption metrics like BLEU, METEOR, CIDEr, and SPICE evaluate quality by comparing generated sentences to human references.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the role of cross-attention in Transformer-based captioning?

options.text: It maps text generation steps directly to visual patch tokens.
It crops text regions from the page.
It converts RGB images to grayscale.

exercises.explain: Correct! Cross-attention enables the text generation layers to pull features from the spatial image representations.

cards.title: Grading creative work
cards.body: How do you evaluate if a caption model is good? If the model predicts 'A dog running on grass' and the human reference says 'A puppy playing in a field', the model is correct. But standard pixel or exact string matching would score this as a complete failure. We need metrics that understand language.

cards.title: Core Evaluation Metrics
cards.body:
- BLEU: Measures n-gram overlap (precision of word sequences).
- METEOR: Includes word stem matching and synonyms.
- CIDEr: Computes TF-IDF weights over human references (weights rare, descriptive words higher).
- SPICE: Converts captions to semantic scene graphs (evaluates objects, attributes, relations).

cards.title: The strict grader vs. the English teacher
cards.body: BLEU is like a strict grader who checks if you used the exact words from the textbook (strict word overlap). SPICE is like an English teacher who reads your essay, maps out the characters and actions, and checks if you captured the overall meaning, regardless of the exact vocabulary you chose.

cards.title: COCO Caption benchmark
cards.body: Researchers use the MS COCO dataset to benchmark caption models. Models are evaluated across multiple human reference captions. While early models focused on BLEU, modern systems prioritize CIDEr and SPICE because they correlate much better with human judgment.

cards.title: BLEU is not the ultimate score
cards.body: You might think a high BLEU score guarantees a great caption. In reality, a model can cheat BLEU by outputting grammatically broken sequences of common words. Relying solely on BLEU can lead to models that score well on paper but sound unnatural to humans.

cards.title: Evaluating Captions
cards.body: Comparing metric focus across candidate captions:

left.text:
Prediction: 'dog dog green grass'
Overlap count is high (BLEU passes)
Human rating: Terrible

right.text:
Prediction: 'A canine plays on lawn'
Zero exact word matches (BLEU fails)
Semantic graph matches (SPICE passes)

exercises.prompt: Which evaluation metric converts captions into semantic scene graphs to check relationships?
exercises.explain: SPICE evaluates semantic structures by translating sentences into graphs of objects, attributes, and relationships.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If a generated caption has a low BLEU score because it uses synonyms (like 'puppy' instead of 'dog'), the caption is guaranteed to be poor.
exercises.explain: False. BLEU is sensitive to exact word overlaps. A caption using synonyms can be excellent despite a low BLEU score.

exercises.sentence: CIDEr weights rare, descriptive words higher by computing ___ scores.
exercises.explain: CIDEr uses TF-IDF vectorization to assign more weight to descriptive, informative words in reference captions.

exercises.prompt: Which of these describes a metric evaluating n-gram overlap precision?
exercises.explain: BLEU evaluates precision by calculating the percentage of predicted n-grams present in references.

exercises.prompt: You are building a photo describer app for children's books. The descriptions must be semantically accurate and capture the relations of characters, but you do not care if the model uses synonyms (like 'bunny' instead of 'rabbit'). Which evaluation metric should you prioritize during validation?

options.text: SPICE, because it evaluates the semantic scene graph (relations and attributes) rather than strict word matching.
BLEU-1, because it only checks for exact single-word matches.
Intersection over Union (IoU) of the pixels.
The loss of the learning rate.

exercises.explain: Exactly! SPICE focuses on semantic correctness (concepts and actions) rather than vocabulary overlaps, making it ideal for tasks where synonym variation is acceptable. Great job! You have finished the Image Captioning Architectures unit. Next up: Neural Style Transfer.
